description: Public-safe security review checklist for 6529bot dogfood expansion, public pre-v1 tags, and changes touching provider calls, GitHub permissions, AWS access, prompt construction, hidden metadata, budget admission, or run control.

phases.title: Scope And Baseline
phases.objective: Anchor the review to a specific candidate and private evidence bundle before evaluating controls.

items.title: Review date, reviewer, commit or tag, target deployment, and release decision scope are recorded privately.
items.evidence: private review header without live credentials or private payloads

items.title: Release-candidate bundle is rendered and reviewed for the security-review candidate.
Operator evidence sections are complete or explicitly deferred with accepted risk.
items.evidence: operator evidence summary, not the private evidence file

items.title: Every security deferral is named in release notes with risk and follow-up ownership.

phases.title: GitHub And Trust Boundaries
phases.objective: Verify GitHub identity, webhook, repository config, and App permission boundaries before model spend.

items.title: Webhooks require valid X-Hub-Signature-256 before parsing and weak-secret warnings are accepted only for non-production.
Production GitHub App settings match the reviewed manifest or an explicitly reviewed manual equivalent.
GitHub App browser handoff routes do not echo manifest codes, generated credentials, payloads, or private repository details.
Public repositories require trusted actors or are disabled before model calls.
Repository config is read from the base ref and can only narrow central policy.
items.evidence: repo config source and merge summary

phases.title: Prompt And Provider Boundaries
phases.objective: Treat all target repo content as untrusted text and keep provider behavior bounded and fail-closed.

items.title: Target repository code is read as text and not executed during review context gathering.
Path reads reject absolute paths, parent traversal, .git paths, directories, and symlinks.
Prompts clearly label diffs, files, commits, and comments as untrusted context.
Provider calls have bounded input, output, timeout, and changed-file limits.
Provider errors are sanitized and empty provider output fails closed before comment posting.
items.evidence: error redaction and empty-output guard summary

phases.title: Spend And Run Control
phases.objective: Verify spend controls and duplicate/concurrency controls run before worker dispatch and provider calls.

items.title: Runtime pause controls are evaluated before budget admission, run-control claims, worker dispatch, and provider calls.
Budget admission happens before queueing model jobs.
Central DB budget policy rows are reviewed, applied from operator-owned files, and loaded before admission.
Run-control claims happen before worker dispatch and dedupe keys include provider and model.
Central worker dispatch uses short-lived GitHub App installation tokens where possible and avoids target-repo long-lived tokens.

phases.title: AWS Ledger And Pricing
phases.objective: Verify AWS access, schema, usage ledger behavior, and model pricing evidence before release.

items.title: AWS IAM/OIDC policies are rendered from reviewed least-privilege templates or equivalent private policy artifacts.
items.evidence: AWS policy review summary without live account ids or ARNs

items.title: Ledger schema changes are reflected in npm run ledger:schema and reviewed before applying to Aurora.
Usage ledger failure mode is explicit and appropriate for the release stage.
Model pricing rows include source URLs, fresh source-checked timestamps, and reviewed stale/zero-price overrides.

phases.title: Public And Private Data Exposure
phases.objective: Verify public outputs are intentionally public and private admin outputs are authenticated, bounded, and sanitized.

items.title: Public usage summaries redact private repo names unless allowlisted.
Admin usage routes fail closed unless the 6529.io auth bridge authorizes the request.
items.evidence: admin auth bridge mode and required-role summary

items.title: Admin raw events, job events, run claims, and runtime status expose bounded fields and no raw prompts, provider payloads, or webhook bodies.
Preflight, admin runtime status, and support bundles report secret presence or missing configuration only, never secret values.

phases.title: Workflow And Release Artifacts
phases.objective: Verify committed workflows and public artifacts keep release-time security boundaries intact.

items.title: Workflow permissions are minimal and third-party actions are pinned by commit SHA.
Reusable workflow callers map only declared provider secrets and do not use secrets: inherit.
Public docs, configs, templates, workflows, and durable memory pass release public-artifact checks.
items.evidence: npm run check:public-artifacts and npm run check:env-templates summaries

phases.title: Operations And Decision
phases.objective: Verify operators can observe, contain, and make a clear release decision.

items.title: Alerting paths do not include secrets, raw prompts, raw diffs, provider payloads, or unsafe custom keys.
Incident response covers spend spikes, secret exposure, provider outages, webhook abuse, ledger outages, and bad bot comments.
Security review decision is recorded as approve, approve with follow-up, defer, or block.
items.evidence: private decision summary and public-safe release note language
